message:
rich_rust-pax,9l7: add link_id and meta fields to Style struct

- Add link_id field for OSC 8 hyperlink tracking/deduplication
- Add meta field for arbitrary serialized metadata storage
- Add link_with_id() and link_id() builder methods
- Add meta() builder method
- Update render() to output OSC 8 with id parameter when set
- Update render_ansi() similarly for hyperlink-only styles
- Update combine() to merge both new fields
- Update Display trait to show link_id in output
- Add 19 comprehensive tests for link_id and meta functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-2ah","title":"Verify API Ergonomics and Rust Idioms","description":"Review and improve API ergonomics to ensure idiomatic Rust usage.

PURPOSE:
Ensure rich_rust provides a pleasant, idiomatic Rust API that follows community conventions and leverages Rust type system effectively.

ERGONOMICS CHECKLIST:

1. BUILDER PATTERNS
- [ ] All complex structs use builder pattern
- [ ] Builders are chainable (return Self)
- [ ] Builders have sensible defaults
- [ ] Terminal build() method returns owned type
- [ ] Example: Table::new().title("X").add_row(...).build()

2. FROM IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] From<&str> for Text
- [ ] From<String> for Text
- [ ] From<&str> for Color (if infallible)
- [ ] TryFrom<&str> for Color (if fallible)
- [ ] From<(u8, u8, u8)> for ColorTriplet
- [ ] Into<Style> for common types

3. DEFAULT IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] All public structs with sensible defaults impl Default
- [ ] Default creates useful zero-config instances
- [ ] Example: Console::default() works reasonably

4. DISPLAY IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] Debug for all public types
- [ ] Display where semantic (Color, Style descriptions)
- [ ] Error types impl std::error::Error

5. CLONE/COPY SEMANTICS
- [ ] Small value types are Copy (ColorTriplet)
- [ ] Larger types are Clone
- [ ] Consider Cow<str> for string-heavy types

6. GENERIC PARAMETERS
- [ ] Functions accept impl AsRef<str> where appropriate
- [ ] Use Into<T> for owned conversions
- [ ] Avoid overly generic signatures that hurt error messages

7. ERROR HANDLING
- [ ] Custom error types with helpful messages
- [ ] Errors implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Consider anyhow/thiserror patterns

8. ITERATORS
- [ ] Types that contain collections expose iterators
- [ ] IntoIterator implementations
- [ ] Iterator adapters where useful

FILES TO REVIEW:
- All public API surfaces in src/*.rs
- src/prelude.rs exports

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- API feels natural to Rust developers
- No clippy warnings on API usage
- Documentation examples compile cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:25.444405212-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:54.100141652-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:54.100141652-05:00","close_reason":"VERIFIED: API follows Rust idioms excellently. Findings: (1) Builder patterns extensively used (Style, Console, Table, Column, Panel, Columns, Align, PrintOptions) with #[must_use] annotations. (2) From/TryFrom implementations comprehensive (Color, ColorTriplet, Style, Text, PaddingDimensions). (3) Default implemented on all public structs. (4) Display/Debug on all appropriate types. (5) Clone/Copy correctly categorized. (6) Error types implement std::error::Error. (7) 133+ methods use builder pattern. The API is well-designed and idiomatic."}
 {"id":"rich_rust-2oy","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Examples","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for rich_rust users.

## Background
Good documentation is essential for adoption. This includes:
1. API documentation (rustdoc)
2. User guide with examples
3. Migration guide from Python Rich
4. Example programs

## Scope

### API Documentation
- Module-level documentation
- Struct/function documentation
- Code examples in doc comments

### User Guide
- Getting started
- Core concepts (Console, Style, Text, Segment)
- Renderables (Table, Panel, Tree, etc.)
- Advanced topics

### Examples
- Basic usage
- Tables
- Panels
- Progress bars
- Custom renderables

### Migration Guide
- Python Rich -> rich_rust mapping
- Feature parity table
- Known differences

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs documented
- Examples compile and run
- User can learn from docs without source code","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. Examples work. Migration guide complete.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:09.333195865-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:09.333195865-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-33q","title":"EPIC: Test Coverage & Quality Assurance","description":"## Objective
Achieve comprehensive test coverage for all modules, ensuring spec compliance and preventing regressions.

## Background
Test coverage is essential for:
1. Verifying spec compliance
2. Preventing regressions during refactoring
3. Documenting expected behavior
4. Enabling confident changes

## Scope

### Unit Tests
- Color parsing and conversion
- Style combination and ANSI rendering
- Segment operations
- Text wrapping and division
- Markup parsing
- Measurement protocol
- Cell width calculations

### Integration Tests
- Console rendering pipeline
- Table layout and rendering
- Panel rendering
- Nested renderables

### Property-Based Tests
- Text wrapping with arbitrary input
- Color conversion roundtrips
- Style combination associativity

### Edge Case Tests
- Wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- Zero-width characters
- Empty inputs
- Very large inputs
- Unicode edge cases

## Success Criteria
- >90% code coverage for core modules
- All spec requirements have corresponding tests
- No known failing tests
- Property tests pass with thousands of cases","acceptance_criteria":"Code coverage >90%. All spec requirements tested.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:16.704297277-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:08:29.192820683-05:00"}
-{"id":"rich_rust-3oo","title":"Console missing render_lines() helper method","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 7.6, Console should have a `render_lines()` helper method that renders content to list of lines, each line being a list of segments.

This method is useful for:
- Table cell rendering
- Panel/box content wrapping
- Layouts that need line-by-line access

## Signature from Spec
```rust
fn render_lines(
    &self,
    renderable: &dyn Renderable,
    options: &ConsoleOptions,
    style: Option<&Style>,
    pad: bool,
    new_lines: bool,
) -> Vec<Vec<Segment>>
```

## Fix
Implement render_lines() method in Console struct following the spec.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:52.166325908-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:05.982735515-05:00","labels":["console","implementation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-4ot","title":"Set Up Golden File Test Infrastructure","description":"Create golden file (snapshot) testing infrastructure for visual regression detection.

PURPOSE:
Golden file tests capture expected rendering output and detect visual regressions by comparing actual output against stored expectations. This is CRITICAL for a terminal rendering library.

INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIREMENTS:

1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE:
   tests/golden/
   - fixtures/           (Input data for tests)
   - expected/           (Golden output files)
   - golden_test.rs      (Test runner)

2. TEST RUNNER FEATURES:
   - Compare actual vs expected output
   - Strip ANSI codes option for text-only comparison
   - Generate diff on failure
   - Auto-update mode (GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 env var)
   - Detailed failure messages with line-by-line diff

3. REQUIRED GOLDEN FILES (minimum):
   - table_basic.golden
   - table_styled.golden
   - panel_simple.golden
   - panel_nested.golden
   - text_wrapped.golden
   - rule_variants.golden
   - tree_nested.golden
   - columns_layout.golden

4. IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS:
   - Use insta crate for snapshot testing
   - OR custom implementation with file comparison

5. LOGGING:
   - Use tracing on comparison functions
   - Log: test name, expected path, lengths
   - On failure: log diff location

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Golden file infrastructure compiles and runs
- At least 8 golden files created
- Auto-update mode works
- Failures produce readable diffs
- CI integration documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:43.197631197-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","close_reason":"Golden file test infrastructure complete: created tests/golden_test.rs with 31 snapshot tests covering Rule (4), Panel (6), Table (7), Tree (3), Columns (2), ProgressBar (4), and combined/edge-case tests (5). Generated 31 snapshot files in tests/snapshots/. All 37 tests pass.","labels":["critical","infrastructure","testing"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-4s3","title":"Validate Measurement Protocol against RICH_SPEC Section 5","description":"## Objective
Verify the Measurement implementation in src/measure.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 5 exactly.

## Background
The Measurement protocol determines how much horizontal space a renderable needs. It returns minimum and maximum widths, enabling the layout system to calculate optimal column widths for tables and panels.

## Validation Checklist

### 5.1 Measurement Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: minimum (usize), maximum (usize)
- [ ] Verify invariant: minimum <= maximum always maintained
- [ ] Verify Measurement::new(minimum, maximum) constructor
- [ ] Verify constructor normalizes if minimum > maximum

### 5.2 Measurement Methods
- [ ] Verify normalize() ensures minimum <= maximum
- [ ] Verify with_maximum(width) clamps both values
- [ ] Verify with_minimum(width) sets floor for both values
- [ ] Verify clamp(min, max) constrains to range
- [ ] Verify union(other) takes max of maximums, min of minimums
- [ ] Verify span accessor returns maximum - minimum

### 5.3 Utility Functions
- [ ] Verify measure_union(measurements) unions all measurements
- [ ] Verify measure_sum(measurements) sums all measurements
- [ ] Verify get_measurement returns Measurement for any Measurable

### 5.4 Measurable Trait (if implemented)
- [ ] Verify trait signature: fn measure(&self, max_width: usize) -> Measurement
- [ ] Verify implementations for: Text, Segment, Table, Panel

### 5.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test measurement with zero width content
- [ ] Test measurement with very large content
- [ ] Test clamp with inverted min/max

## Files to Examine
- src/measure.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 5","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Invariant maintenance confirmed.","notes":"Validation complete: Measurement struct, Measurement::get, and measure_renderables match RICH_SPEC Section 5; no code changes needed. Blocked only by epic rich_rust-q1k closure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.545908076-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:37.916657579-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:37.916657579-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete per existing notes: Measurement struct, Measurement::get, and measure_renderables match RICH_SPEC Section 5; no code changes needed.","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-4s3","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.860321866-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-85g","title":"Verify Hyperlink (OSC 8) Support","description":"## Objective
Verify and complete hyperlink support using OSC 8 escape sequences.

## Background
Modern terminals support clickable hyperlinks via OSC 8 escape sequences. The Style struct has a link field, but rendering may be incomplete.

## Technical Details

### OSC 8 Format
```
ESC ] 8 ; params ; URI ST ... text ... ESC ] 8 ; ; ST
```
Where:
- ESC ] = 0x1B 0x5D (OSC introducer)
- ST = 0x1B 0x5C (string terminator) or 0x07 (BEL)
- params = optional key=value pairs (e.g., id=unique-id)
- URI = the link URL

### Example
```
\x1b]8;;https://example.com\x07Click here\x1b]8;;\x07
```

### Verification Checklist
- [ ] Style::link(url) stores URL correctly
- [ ] Style parsing handles 'link=URL' syntax
- [ ] ANSI rendering produces correct OSC 8 sequence
- [ ] Links work in markup: [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] Nested styles with links render correctly
- [ ] Terminal compatibility: iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal

### Implementation Gaps (if found)
- May need to add OSC 8 rendering to style.rs render_to_ansi()
- May need console.rs to check terminal hyperlink support

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (link field, ANSI rendering)
- src/console.rs (terminal capability detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.2","acceptance_criteria":"Hyperlinks render correctly. Tested in compatible terminals.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:03.561080644-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","close_reason":"Hyperlink (OSC 8) support verified. Created comprehensive test suite (30+ tests) covering: Style::link() storage, parsing, ANSI rendering with correct OSC 8 sequences, markup integration, style combination, and edge cases. All tests pass.","labels":["implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:54.797298549-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.03192436-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-8cf","title":"Add Unit Tests for Style Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/style.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Style Construction
- [ ] Style::null() - empty style
- [ ] Style::new() - same as null
- [ ] Builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), etc.
- [ ] color() and on() for foreground/background

### Attributes
- [ ] Individual attribute flags
- [ ] Combined attributes: bold + italic
- [ ] set() and unset() methods
- [ ] is_set() checks

### Style Combination
- [ ] null + style = style
- [ ] style + null = style
- [ ] style1 + style2 (attribute union)
- [ ] Color override (second wins)
- [ ] Associativity: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)

### Style Parsing
- [ ] Single attribute: 'bold'
- [ ] Single color: 'red'
- [ ] Compound: 'bold red on blue'
- [ ] With link: 'link=https://example.com'
- [ ] With 'not': 'not bold'
- [ ] Invalid inputs

### ANSI Rendering
- [ ] Null style produces reset
- [ ] Single attribute
- [ ] Foreground color (all systems)
- [ ] Background color (all systems)
- [ ] Combined style

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/style.rs (add tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for style.rs. Combination semantics verified.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Log style inputs at DEBUG level
3. Log combination results at DEBUG level
4. Log ANSI output at TRACE level (can be verbose)

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_style_combine() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let bold = Style::new().bold();
    let red = Style::new().color(Color::Red);
    
    debug!(style1 = ?bold, style2 = ?red, "Combining styles");
    let combined = bold.combine(&red);
    debug!(result = ?combined, "Combined style");
    
    assert!(combined.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    info!("Style combination PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:45.709080256-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:42.850382603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:42.850382603-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8cf","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.680310869-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented LRU caching for cell_len per RICH_SPEC Section 12.4. Added thread-safe LazyLock<Mutex<LruCache>> with 1024 entries. Strings < 8 chars bypass cache for efficiency. Added cell_len_uncached() for direct access. All 424 tests pass.","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.1, the Style struct should have a `meta: Option<Vec<u8>>` field for serialized metadata.

Current implementation is missing this field entirely.

## Impact
Very low - this field is used in Python Rich for storing arbitrary metadata attached to styles. Not commonly used.

 {"id":"rich_rust-a62","title":"Verify Style Parsing Edge Cases","description":"## Objective
Ensure Style::parse() handles all edge cases from RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.

## Background
Style parsing converts strings like 'bold red on blue' into Style structs. Edge cases include empty strings, invalid keywords, and complex combinations.

## Technical Details

### Test Cases from Spec

#### Valid Inputs
- '' (empty) → null style
- 'bold' → bold attribute only
- 'red' → red foreground only
- 'on blue' → blue background only
- 'bold italic red on blue' → all combined
- 'not bold' → unset bold
- '#ff0000' → hex color foreground
- 'rgb(255,0,0)' → RGB color foreground
- 'color(128)' → 256-color foreground
- 'link=https://example.com' → hyperlink

#### Invalid Inputs (should error or ignore)
- 'invalid' → unknown keyword
- 'bold bold' → duplicate attribute
- 'on' → incomplete background
- 'red blue' → ambiguous (two foregrounds?)

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each valid input
2. Write test for each invalid input
3. Compare error handling to Python Rich
4. Verify error messages are helpful

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (parse method)
- Python Rich style.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.4","acceptance_criteria":"All edge cases handled correctly. Error messages are helpful.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:04:42.788769707-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:04:42.788769707-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive test suite (56 tests) for Style::parse() edge cases. Fixed bug where BLINK2, FRAME, and ENCIRCLE attributes were silently ignored during parsing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.886973112-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.941197777-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a96","title":"EPIC: Implementation Refinement & Gap Closure","description":"## Objective
Address identified gaps between current implementation and RICH_SPEC requirements, plus implement missing features discovered during validation.

## Background
During spec validation, several gaps and missing features were identified. This epic tracks all implementation work needed to achieve full spec compliance.

## Scope

### Critical Gaps (Must Fix)
1. LRU caching for color parsing - spec requires caching for performance
2. Text.divide() algorithm - must match Python Rich exactly
3. Ratio distribution algorithm - verify integer rounding behavior
4. Hyperlink support (OSC 8) - may be incomplete

### Potential Gaps (Verify First)
1. Style parsing edge cases
2. Markup parser error recovery
3. Table width collapse algorithm
4. Wide character handling in cell calculations

### Implementation Improvements
1. API ergonomics (builder patterns, From impls)
2. Error messages and handling
3. Thread safety considerations

## Success Criteria
- All RICH_SPEC requirements implemented
- No behavioral differences from Python Rich (for in-scope features)
- Clean, idiomatic Rust code

## Dependencies

 {"id":"rich_rust-ag4","title":"Create E2E Test: Text Wrapping and Justification","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for text wrapping, justification, and the Text.divide() algorithm.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Word Wrap
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_word_wrap() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let text = Text::plain("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
        .wrap(20);
    
    let lines: Vec<_> = text.iter().collect();
    debug!(line_count = lines.len(), "Wrapped text");
    
    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {
        let width = line.cell_length();
        debug!(line = i, width = width, content = %line, "Line detail");
        assert!(width <= 20, "Line {} exceeds width: {}", i, width);
    }
    
    info!("Word wrap test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Long Word Breaking
- Word longer than width
- Verify: Word is broken, not truncated

### Scenario 3: Styled Text Wrapping
- Text with style spans
- Verify: Styles preserved across line breaks

### Scenario 4: Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_text_divide() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());  // 'world' is bold
    
    debug!(original = %text.plain, spans = ?text.spans, "Original text");
    
    let divided = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    
    for (i, part) in divided.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            part = i,
            content = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Verify spans are correctly split
    assert_eq!(divided[0].plain, "hello");
    assert!(divided[0].spans.is_empty(), "First part should have no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[1].plain, " worl");
    assert!(!divided[1].spans.is_empty(), "Second part should have bold span");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[2].plain, "d!");
    assert!(!divided[2].spans.is_empty(), "Third part should have bold span");
    
    info!("Text.divide test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 5: Justification
- Left, Right, Center, Full justify
- Verify: Correct padding/spacing

### Scenario 6: Wide Characters
- CJK text wrapping
- Emoji handling
- Verify: Cell width respected

### Scenario 7: Overflow Methods
- Fold, Crop, Ellipsis
- Verify: Each method works correctly

## Logging Requirements
- Original text and spans
- Wrap width and resulting lines
- Divide offsets and resulting parts
- Span transformations during divide
- Cell width calculations

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_text.rs
- tests/golden/text_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All text scenarios pass. divide() algorithm verified with span preservation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:03.615570733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:47.004590631-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:47.004590631-05:00","close_reason":"E2E Text Wrapping tests complete with 41 passing tests covering all scenarios","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.972287839-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.099529002-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

- [ ] Verify SQUARE box: sharp corners (┌┐└┘)

- [ ] Verify MINIMAL box: minimal borders
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE box: simple lines

- [ ] Verify get_bottom() returns bottom row chars
- [ ] Verify substitute(safe_box) for ASCII fallback

- [ ] Verify non-ASCII terminals get ASCII automatically

 {"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Verify the Segment implementation in src/segment.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 3 exactly.

## Background
Segment is the atomic rendering unit - a piece of text with a single style. The rendering pipeline produces streams of segments. Control segments handle terminal manipulation (cursor, clear, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Verify Segment::line() creates newline segment
- [ ] Verify Segment::control(codes) creates control segment

### 3.2 ControlType Enum

- [ ] Verify ControlCode struct with control_type and params fields

### 3.3 Segment Methods
- [ ] Verify is_control() returns true iff control is Some
- [ ] Verify cell_length() returns 0 for control segments
- [ ] Verify cell_length() uses Unicode cell width for text
- [ ] Verify is_empty() checks both text and control
- [ ] Verify split_at_cell(pos) splits at cell position (not byte)

### 3.4 Segment Operations (Functions)
- [ ] Verify apply_style(segments, style, post_style)
- [ ] Verify split_lines(segments) splits at newlines
- [ ] Verify adjust_line_length(line, length, style, pad)
- [ ] Verify simplify(segments) merges adjacent same-style
- [ ] Verify divide(segments, cuts) divides at cell positions
- [ ] Verify align_top/bottom/middle vertical alignment

### 3.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test split_at_cell with wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- [ ] Test divide with cuts at wide character boundaries
- [ ] Test simplify preserves control segments

## Files to Examine

- src/cells.rs (cell width calculation)

-{"id":"rich_rust-pax","title":"Style missing link_id field for hyperlink tracking","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.6, the Style struct should have a `link_id: String` field for hyperlink tracking in OSC 8 sequences.

Current implementation:
- `Style` struct is missing the `link_id` field
- Hyperlink output uses empty id: `\x1b]8;;{url}\x1b\\`
- Should be: `\x1b]8;id={link_id};{url}\x1b\\`

## Impact
Low - hyperlinks work without ids, but tracking/deduplication is not possible.

## Fix
1. Add `link_id: Option<String>` to Style struct
2. Generate unique id when link is set (if not provided)
3. Update render() and render_ansi() to include id in OSC 8 sequence","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:38:59.071153066-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:13.84549087-05:00","labels":["implementation","style"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:40.012060914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:40.012060914-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All 15 spec sections validated against RICH_SPEC.md. Verified: Color System (1), Style System (2), Segment Operations (3), Markup Parser (4), Measurement Protocol (5), Text Object (6), Console (7), Box Characters (8), Table Layout (9), Panel & Padding (10), Alignment (11), Unicode Cell Width (12), Text Wrapping (13), Ratio Distribution (14), Exclusions (15). All 273+ tests pass."}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete

### Findings Summary

1. **calculate_widths()** - CORRECT: Main algorithm follows spec structure

2. **expand_widths()** - CRITICAL DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-t54):
   - Distributes extra space equally instead of by column ratio
   - Column.ratio field is completely ignored
   
3. **collapse_widths()** - MINOR DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-gk4):
   - Missing rounding error correction loop from spec
   - May cause off-by-one in edge cases

### Deliverables
- Created tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs with test vectors
- Created bug issues: rich_rust-t54 (P1), rich_rust-gk4 (P2)
- All tests pass

### Test Vector Evidence
- expand_widths: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  - Expected: [30,40,30], Actual: [33,33,34]","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Parser handles all edge cases correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","close_reason":"Markup Parser validation complete - all RICH_SPEC Section 4 requirements verified:
- Tag syntax: [tag], [/], [/tag], [tag=param], [@handler] supported
- Regex pattern: (\\*)\[([a-z#/@][^\[\]]*?)\] matches spec (improved to exclude both brackets)
- Parsing algorithm: optimization for no '[', style stack, escape handling, auto-close unclosed tags
- Error handling: MarkupError with UnmatchedClosingTag and InvalidTag variants
- 17 unit tests covering: plain text, bold, nesting, multiple styles, escaping, unclosed tags, link tags, colors
- render(), render_or_plain(), escape() functions all match spec","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-qgs","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.801842899-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
